conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible. I have two children - Alex, who is 12, and Blake, who is 8.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! You have two children - Alex (12 years old) and Blake (8 years old).

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for each child.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with the children.

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to check if anyone else is currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, in care, or in residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Based on the information you've provided, I have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me review the criteria:

- You live in the UK ✓
- Alex (12) and Blake (8) are both under 16 ✓
- Both children live with you ✓
- You are responsible for them ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them ✓
- Neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation ✓

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Based on your circumstances:
- **Alex (12 years old)**: You are eligible because Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.
- **Blake (8 years old)**: You are eligible because Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive Child Benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit for both children. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with two children, Alex (12) and Blake (8), both under 16 years old. The claimant is responsible for both children who live with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
